stories.headline: Zelensky proposes face-to-face talks in open letter to Putin
stories.summary: Ukraine's president tells the Russian leader that only "direct engagement" between the two countries could end the war, with the US focused on Iran.

stories.headline: Zelenskyy to meet Macron, Merz and Starmer in London on Sunday, France says
stories.summary: Ukraine recaptured more territory than it lost to Russian forces in May for the second straight month, an analysis of data from the Institute for the Study of War carried out by the AFP news agency showed earlier this month.

stories.headline: Are the US and Iran closer to war or to a deal?
stories.summary: As US and Iran pursue talks, both signal they are still ready to use force against each other.

stories.headline: Ukraine and Russia exchange 185 prisoners of war each
stories.summary: Russia and Ukraine have each released 185 prisoners in a major exchange, which Russia says was mediated by the UAE.

stories.headline: Israel maintains attacks despite US-brokered deal with Lebanon
stories.summary: The Israeli army also issues new forced displacement orders for several towns and villages in southern Lebanon.

stories.headline: Xi Jinping to meet Kim Jong Un in rare visit to North Korea
stories.summary: It is taking place weeks after Xi met the leaders of the US and Russia - two countries that loom large over North Korea's foreign policy.

stories.headline: Investors boost bets for Fed rate rise after bumper US jobs report
stories.summary: World’s biggest economy adds 172,000 jobs in May in sign labour market is ‘turning the corner’

stories.headline: Inside the CBS mutiny against Bari Weiss and David Ellison
stories.summary: A saga that started with Donald Trump’s assault on ‘60 Minutes’ has turned into a full-blown crisis

stories.headline: Morgan Stanley Sees AI-Related Funding Expanding to 15% of All Credit Deals
stories.summary: Scott Goodwin, co-founder and managing partner at Diameter Capital Partners, says that business development companies will face pressure to sell their highest-quality loans to software companies at a discount as banks tighten their grip on the private credit funds. Goodwin speaks with Bloomberg TV's Dani Burger at the Bloomberg Global Credit Forum in New York on June 3. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Credit Titans Warn of Shakeout on Debt Deals That ‘Don’t Make Sense’
stories.summary: Davidson Kempner's Suzanne Gibbons, Glendon Capital's Holly Kim, Sculptor Capital's Brett Klein and Matthew Rahmani of Perella Weinberg speak with Bloomberg News' Irene Garcia Perez on how credit markets are navigating heightened volatility, higher-for-longer interest rates and the credit market's ``cockroach'' problem at Bloomberg's Global Credit Forum in New York on June 3. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: TAG Immobilien Is Said to Eye Polish IPO Kickoff in Coming Weeks
stories.summary: German real estate group TAG Immobilien AG is considering starting the formal process for a potential initial public offering of its Polish unit Robyg SA in Warsaw as soon as the coming weeks, according to people familiar with the matter.

stories.headline: EU Calls on Spain to Cut Gas Use for Stability Post-Blackout
stories.summary: The European Commission has called on Spain to move away from using gas-fired power plants to stabilize its electricity system after last year’s blackout, saying better grids, interconnections and more storage should replace the emergency measures.

stories.headline: Spelman College Names Ayanna Howard, an A.I. Expert, as President
stories.summary: The appointment of Ayanna Howard, a roboticist, was a departure for the top-ranked H.B.C.U., which has a liberal arts focus.

stories.headline: Typhoon Jangmi sweeps northwards leaving 23 injured in Japan
stories.summary: More than 1 million people advised to evacuate homes amid 80mph winds and heavy rainTyphoon Jangmi (also known as Typhoon No 6) moved northwards over the course of this week. From Okinawa to mainland Japan, prolonged and heavy rainfall led to landslide warnings and the flooding of rivers, with Japan issuing level 4 warnings for some rivers, signalling a risk of overflowing. This level is high enough for municipalities to issue evacuation orders.

stories.headline: EU must prove it is capable and willing to take in new members, leaders say
stories.summary: Von der Leyen tells Balkans summit that bloc needs to make enlargement process ‘faster and more credible’The EU must prove its willingness and ability to take in new members and speed up its enlargement process, leaders of the bloc have said, as they gathered with their counterparts from six western Balkan countries that hope to join soon.“The European Union has to show that it is capable of enlarging and willing to enlarge, and we want to discus

stories.headline: Barcelona teenager Lamine Yamal wins La Liga Player of the Season award
stories.summary: Lamine Yamal will be an integral part of Spain's World Cup 2026 challenge and was key in Barcelona's La Liga defence.

stories.headline: China's Xi to visit North Korea next week, first time since 2019
Albania's Edi Rama tells Euronews there is 'no alternative' to EU accession
stories.summary: In a wide-ranging interview with Euronews, Albanian Prime Minister Edi Rama said there is no alternative for his country to EU accession. He also warned about the risks posed by anti-EU forces and “third actors” operating in the Western Balkans.

stories.headline: Rama alleges 'hybrid war' behind protests against Kushner-linked coastal development
stories.summary: The luxury venture, spanning a protected island and a stretch of seafront on the southern coast, has drawn opposition from environmental campaigners and critics of Prime Minister Edi Rama, who sees external interference.

stories.headline: Why Portugal loses 'about half of its lifeguards' each beach season
stories.summary: Portugal’s lifeguards' federation warns of too few lifeguards patrolling beaches, blaming low pay, poor job security and lack of strategy.

stories.headline: Norway's crown princess on lung transplant waiting list, palace says
stories.summary: Mette-Marit's condition, which stiffens the lungs, making it hard to breathe, has deteriorated, the royal household says.

stories.headline: China cracks down on soft porn, violence and materialism in viral micro dramas
stories.summary: Micro dramas have surged in popularity, but drawn criticism for often sensationalist content.

stories.headline: Nasdaq tumbles 3% as shares in chip and memory groups sink
stories.summary: Rising expectations of Federal Reserve rate rise send US bond yields rising sharply

stories.headline: Pugilistic Florida billionaire plots law firm stake sale to private equity
stories.summary: Move by largest US personal injury firm could cement buyout groups’ arrival in a sector that has long been off-limits

stories.headline: Trump lawyers refuse to reveal financial information to BBC in $10bn lawsuit
stories.summary: Broadcaster has sought documents under subpoena in effort to identify impact of ‘Panorama’ documentary on US president

stories.headline: US demands tougher rules for car parts in Mexico trade talks
stories.summary: Washington wants to reduce Chinese supplies from North America’s vehicles while increasing American content

stories.headline: How to Make Sure You Have Enough Money for Retirement
stories.summary: How much money do you really need to last for your retirement? It's a question investment professionals deal with on a daily basis. Gabriela Santos, JPMorgan Asset Management chief market strategist for Americas, says as Americans live longer, they have to "plan to get to and through retirement." She appears on the debut episode of "Bloomberg Money." (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: TPG, Oaktree, Oak Hill Lead €1 Billion Debt for Evoke Buyout
stories.summary: Private lenders including TPG Credit, Oaktree Capital Management and Oak Hill Advisors are set to provide around €1 billion ($1.2 billion) of funding for the acquisition of gambling firm Evoke Plc by Bally’s Intralot.

stories.headline: AstraZeneca Broadens US Growth Push
stories.summary: AstraZeneca CFO Aradhana Sarin discusses how the pharmaceutical company is broadening its US shareholder base and plans to generate $80 Billion in revenue by 2030. She speaks with Bloomberg's Katie Greifeld. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: SpaceX IPO Is Said to Draw More Orders Than Shares Available
stories.summary: SpaceX has already received orders for more than the shares available in its $75 billion initial public offering, according to people familiar with the matter, putting the company on the verge of setting the record for the biggest-ever listing.

stories.headline: Chuck Schumer Privately Backs Haley Stevens in Michigan Democratic Senate Primary
stories.summary: Chuck Schumer, the Democratic minority leader, has remained officially neutral on the race, but in conversations with donors, he has been clear about which candidate he supports.

stories.headline: Casting Her 10,000th Vote in a Row, Collins Sets a Senate Record
stories.summary: The Maine Republican, who is in the middle of a tumultuous re-election race, became the first senator in history to reach the threshold without missing a vote.

stories.headline: House Passes Ukraine Aid in Defiance of Republican Leaders
stories.summary: Eighteen G.O.P. lawmakers broke with their party and joined Democrats to deliver yet another blow to the president’s foreign policy agenda.

stories.headline: Australian housing was already cooling before the budget – but how cold it gets depends on two key factors
stories.summary: Most economists believe the chronic undersupply of homes will eventually push prices higher once interest rates ease and the tax changes are priced inGet our breaking news email, free app or daily news podcastThe government’s property tax changes have become one of the defining political issues of Labor’s second term, drawing fierce criticism from opponents who argue they represent an “assault on aspiration” that will destroy home values.In the t

stories.headline: More than a quarter of UK musicians lost all EU work since 2021, report finds
stories.summary: Average tour earnings down 45%, with nearly three-fifths of musicians saying touring in Europe is no longer viableMore than a quarter of British musicians have lost all their work in the European Union since 2021, according to new research.The report by European Movement UK, a cross-party campaign group advocating closer UK-EU relations, found that nearly half of British musicians had experienced a reduced amount of work in the EU since 2021, whi

stories.headline: Somalia declares order restored after two days of fighting in Mogadishu
stories.summary: Violence that paralysed two Mogadishu districts subsides, but opposition rejects president's rule and vows to fight on.

stories.headline: Japan's farmer-backed ruling party to support trade talks with Mercosur
Canadian miner taps Brazil to carve China out of rare-earth supply chain
China pulled into public debate over subsidies by OECD report
South Korean stocks fall, currency slips as foreigners dump shares
AI glasses maker Rokid lands Toyota affiliate investment
Israeli strikes kill 11 people in Gaza City, medics say
stories.summary: Women and children were among those killed in the attacks on four residential buildings, which Israel's military says killed four Hamas security officials.

stories.headline: Israel and Lebanon agree to implement ceasefire if Hezbollah stops attacks
stories.summary: The countries reject "any attempt, by any state or non-state actor, to hold Lebanon's future hostage", the US says.

stories.headline: Japan sees shortage of plastic bags, trays and gloves, as Iran war-induced naphtha shortage worsens
stories.summary: The Middle East is Japan’s main source of crude oil, from which naphtha is extracted and used to make items including printing ink and plasticsTakeaways, supermarkets, and bakeries in Japan are running out of plastic bags, trays and food service gloves amid widening shortages of the key plastic ingredient, naphtha, due to the Middle East crisis.The food sector accounts for nearly one-third of Japan’s annual plastic use of more than 8m tonnes, and

stories.headline: Kushner-backed luxury resort plan sparks protests in Albania
stories.summary: Demonstrators say the project backed by Donald Trump's son-in-law would harm a protected environment.

stories.headline: Fifa bans all water bottles from World Cup games
stories.summary: Fifa has been warned it is putting fans' health at risk by introducing a bottle ban at this summer's World Cup.

stories.headline: Miracle on Everest: Guide believed dead spotted crawling down ice
stories.summary: Cleaners found Dawa Sherpa crawling towards Base Camp six days after he went missing at a higher altitude.

stories.headline: Gold Slumps Most in Two Months as Jobs Fuel Fed Rate-Hike Bets
stories.summary: Gold fell the most in more than two months as robust US jobs data fueled bets that the Federal Reserve will likely raise interest rates this year, a headwind for the precious metal.

stories.headline: Here's why counting votes in California is taking so long
stories.summary: It could take weeks - or even a month - before we know who won elections in California and Los Angeles.

stories.headline: Bondi Beach shooting hero charged with assault
stories.summary: Ahmed al Ahmed disarmed one of the alleged shooters during the deadly attack at a Jewish event.

stories.headline: Delcy Rodríguez visits India: Will oil talks lead to an energy deal?
stories.summary: Delcy Rodríguez's India visit highlights Venezuela's growing role in Delhi's oil diversification.

stories.headline: Protesters topple World Cup player statues in Mexico City
stories.summary: Teachers demanding higher pay have been demonstrating for several days and threaten to disrupt the tournament if their demands are not met.

stories.headline: Nasdaq 100 Sinks 3% as Fed-Hike Wagers Lift Yields: Markets Wrap
stories.summary: Wall Street’s historic weekly run came to a halt, with stocks hit by a tech selloff and higher bond yields as a solid jobs report added to bets the Federal Reserve’s next rate move will be a hike.

stories.headline: Canada endorses embattled marine park’s plan to relocate 30 beluga whales
stories.summary: Beluga whales, which Marineland threatened to euthanize in 2025, will be moved to sanctuaries in Spain or across USCanada and an embattled marine park have reached a tentative deal on the future of 30 beluga whales, ending a saga that has captivated the public and angered animal rights groups.The federal fisheries ministry announced this week that all of Marineland’s belugas would be shipped to either Spain or one of four locations in the US, end

stories.headline: Jamaican police officer charged with murder after woman shot during protest
stories.summary: Andrew Wilson appears in court over killing of Latoya Bulgin at protest over a police shooting days earlierAuthorities in Jamaica have taken the rare step of charging a police officer with murder after he was accused of shooting a 45-year-old woman in a case that prompted violent protests.According to the Independent Commission of Investigations (Indecom), Constable Andrew Wilson appeared in court on Wednesday and was denied bail. Another hearing

stories.headline: BYD is losing its spark
stories.summary: The Chinese EV giant is struggling to stay ahead in an industry defined by software

stories.headline: Grab what you can while you can: The new reality in the South China Sea
stories.summary: After years of watching China create land to back its expansive claims, others are doing the same.

stories.headline: Costly fuel pushes more Indians to buy electric cars but challenges remain
stories.summary: High fuel prices are strengthening the case for EV adoption in the world's third largest auto market.

stories.headline: Canada formally requests 16-year renewal of North American free trade pact
stories.summary: In a letter to his North American counterparts, US-Canada Trade Minister Dominic LeBlanc said the three countries should renew the USMCA trade deal.

stories.headline: Putin remains uncompromising on Ukraine, but is public discourse on war changing in Russia?
stories.summary: Russia is intensifying attacks in Ukraine but more than four years of war are causing concern even among Putin loyalists.

stories.headline: Clashes continue in Lebanon despite Israel and Hezbollah accepting US partial ceasefire plan
stories.summary: A cessation of hostilities between Israel and Hezbollah is seen as crucial to the peace process with Iran.

stories.headline: Wars, sanctions and punishments: Iraq's 40-year World Cup wait
stories.summary: Iraq return to the World Cup with ambitions of delivering stronger performances and results than first time round in 1986.

stories.headline: Video shows elaborate drug-smuggling tunnel between US and Mexico
stories.summary: Four people have been arrested and charged with trafficking more than $45 million in cocaine, according to federal officials.

stories.headline: Iran attacks damage 20 US military sites since start of war, satellite images show
stories.summary: Analysts told BBC Verify that Tehran's strikes were more extensive than publicly acknowledged.

stories.headline: Bowen: Trump needs this war to end but Iran is not backing down
stories.summary: Under pressure from the polls and Gulf allies, the White House is pushing for a deal but Iran wants concessions, writes BBC's international editor.

stories.headline: Typhoon Jangmi threatens Japan as Europe swelters
stories.summary: Powerful winds and rain expected in parts of Japan and Australia, while temperatures in Spain could hit 40CA powerful tropical storm is forecast to track near Okinawa, Japan, on Monday before moving towards the south-east of the country. Typhoon Jangmi (also known as Typhoon No 6) has formed within the monsoonal gyre over the Philippine Sea.A monsoonal gyre is a large, slow-rotating weather system that spawns typhoons through smaller vortices for

stories.headline: Watch: Glass doors break as fans scramble to see Pursuit of Jade star
stories.summary: Huge crowds formed outside a shopping centre in China to see actor Zhang Linghe.

stories.headline: Colombia presidential runoff pits leftist senator against pro-Trump rival
stories.summary: Left-wing senator Iván Cepeda will face Trump admirer Abelardo de la Espriella at the final ballot on 21 June.

stories.headline: Israel seizes castle in Lebanon as it expands ground offensive
stories.summary: Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu calls the capture of the strategic fortress a "decisive shift" in Israel's campaign against Hezbollah, as European governments criticise the escalation.

stories.headline: Netanyahu says he has directed IDF to increase control of Gaza to 70%
stories.summary: The expansion in control by Israel would contradict the terms of the ceasefire  Israel and Hamas agreed to in October 2025.

stories.headline: US and Iran 'very close' to deal but 'not there yet', Vance says
stories.summary: US officials earlier told the BBC that the framework of a ceasefire extension deal had been agreed, pending the approval of Trump and Iran's leadership.
